data.title: CLAUDE.md bloat: documentation tasks bypass doc-writer read-only guard and add content without bloat review
data.description:
## Problem

CLAUDE.md has grown to ~12,046 tokens despite two safeguards designed to prevent bloat:

1. **Architectural Invariant 2** (CLAUDE.md line 140): 'CLAUDE.md is for agent instructions, rules, and command references — not feature descriptions.'
2. **doc-writer CLAUDE.md Read-Only Guard** (doc-writer.md §2): The doc-writer agent is instructed to emit suggested-change reports instead of modifying CLAUDE.md directly.

Despite these safeguards, git log shows frequent CLAUDE.md modifications from documentation stories:
- docs(8e7b): update CLAUDE.md with Part C and consumer_completeness documentation
- docs(b555-bac6): document relates_to drift detection in CLAUDE.md
- docs(f06e-56d3): update CLAUDE.md with review decision gate features
- docs(07cb-5477): document deterministic testing scripts in CLAUDE.md
- docs(1f75-9b32): update CLAUDE.md and project docs for test quality enforcement
- docs(f2be-1da6): add config key docs (already in CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md)

## Root Cause Analysis (3 failure modes)

### Failure Mode 1: Narrow doc-story title match bypasses doc-writer
The sprint dispatch table (sprint/SKILL.md:1074) only routes to dso:doc-writer when the task title matches 'Update project docs to reflect'. Tasks with other titles (e.g., 'document X in CLAUDE.md', 'add Y docs') go to a generic sub-agent that lacks the CLAUDE.md read-only guard. This is the primary leak — most CLAUDE.md-modifying commits come from non-matching titles.

### Failure Mode 2: No orchestrator-side bloat gate for CLAUDE.md changes
Even when the doc-writer correctly emits a suggested-change report, the sprint orchestrator has no gate to evaluate whether the change is warranted or whether it would cause bloat. The orchestrator blindly applies suggestions. There is no token budget or threshold check.

### Failure Mode 3: Non-doc tasks also modify CLAUDE.md
Implementation tasks (not doc stories) sometimes add architecture documentation to CLAUDE.md as part of 'completing' their work. The safeguard files protection (Never-Do rule 18) requires 'task-level authorization', which is interpreted broadly — any task that touches a feature area feels authorized to document it in CLAUDE.md.

## Scope of Investigation

1. **doc-writer dispatch reliability** — Is the title-match pattern in sprint SKILL.md (line 1074) catching all documentation tasks? Should the match be broadened, or should ALL tasks that touch CLAUDE.md route through doc-writer regardless of title?

2. **doc-writer guidance adequacy** — The read-only guard correctly prevents direct writes, but the suggested-change mechanism lacks bloat-prevention criteria. The agent should evaluate whether the proposed change belongs in CLAUDE.md vs. a reference doc (CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md, ARCHITECTURE-REFERENCE.md, or the relevant SKILL.md).

3. **Orchestrator-side CLAUDE.md gate** — Should the sprint orchestrator have an explicit gate that blocks CLAUDE.md modifications unless they pass a bloat-review check? This would catch both doc-writer suggestions and direct modifications from non-doc sub-agents.

4. **Content that should be extracted** — ~45% of current CLAUDE.md content is detailed subsystem documentation that duplicates content already in SKILL.md files or reference docs (e.g., config keys are fully documented in CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md, scrutiny pipeline details are in brainstorm SKILL.md, sprint self-healing is in sprint SKILL.md). This content accumulated because there was no gate preventing it.

## Evidence
- Current CLAUDE.md: ~12,046 tokens / 43.3KB
- Config keys paragraph (line 97, ~1,676 chars): fully duplicated in CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md (1,175 lines)
- Scrutiny pipeline details (line 87, ~2,551 chars): 25 matching references already in brainstorm SKILL.md
- Sprint self-healing (line 93, ~1,224 chars): 10 matching references already in sprint SKILL.md
- Proposal generation (line 92, ~718 chars): 35 matching references already in implementation-plan SKILL.md
